name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface

summary.name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface
summary.hypothesis: Round 16 turned the current PTX control branch back into a net negative. The implementation changed only one new hot-band seam, but a source diff versus the best measured PTX commit `489574e` now shows two bounded drifts on the PTX winner surface: the round-15 future_tile_k hoist and the round-16 prologue wait retime. The latest run regressed from 24.17100811 ms to 24.19086361 ms even though barrier dipped slightly from 5.21% to 5.16%, because long-scoreboard widened back from 7.08% to 7.16% and the end-to-end result moved away from both the 24.17859173 ms accepted base and the 24.16427231 ms best-known run. The next move should therefore be to restore the best measured PTX surface before spending more rounds on alternate families.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Source drift away from the proven PTX winner surface, specifically the prologue wait window and refill-address hot-band seam, not a need for another fresh PTX control experiment on top of the regressed variant.
summary.risk: Low to moderate. This is a small, auditable restore onto the best measured PTX surface rather than a new speculative optimization.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.190864 ms current run, the 24.178592 ms accepted base, and the 24.164272 ms best-known run
correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
ranking_notes: This ranks first because the current source is a clearly isolated negative drift away from the PTX winner surface rather than a promising new branch.
The restore is small, auditable, and immediately re-anchors future comparisons after the failed wait-window retime.
It also preserves optionality, because grouped_rows=8 and 256x128 families can still be explored from a known-good surface afterward.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the source already matches the best PTX surface exactly or if the restore fails to recover a cleaner runtime signature on re-measurement.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
